name: Cerro Masatrigo Botanical Trail
name_original: Cerro Masatrigo – running circular por el sendero botánico (SL-BA 198)
description: Jogging the difficult 4.2-mile Cerro Masatrigo botanical trail offers panoramic views of La Serena reservoir around the iconic "Magic Mounta
region_intro: Jogging around Embalse de la Serena offers extensive scenic routes across a vast landscape. This region, home to one of Europe's largest reservoirs, features an "inland sea" feel with boundless horizons. The terrain includes large granite, quartzite, or slate plains, rolling hills, and pastures interspersed with holm oaks. These varied features provide diverse running experiences, from flat stretches to gently undulating paths. Best jogging routes around Embalse de la Serena The most popular jogging route is Cerro Masatrigo Botanical Trail, a 4.2 miles (6.8 km) trail that takes about 50 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers panoramic views around the distinctive conical Cerro Masatrigo. Another top favourite among local runners is Peñón del Pez – running circular from Peñalsordo, a difficult 5.4 miles (8.7 km) path. This route features varied terrain with significant elevation changes, providing a challenging experience. Local runners also love the Collado de Santa Ana loop from Peñalsordo, an 8.3 miles (13.4 km) trail leading through rolling hills and open landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 30 minutes. Jogging around Embalse de la Serena is defined by vast open spaces, rolling hills, and granite plains dotted with holm oaks. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to different ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 10 runners have used komoot to explore Embalse de la Serena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Embalse de la Serena?
A: There are 7 dedicated running routes around Embalse de la Serena, catering to various fitness levels. You'll find options ranging from easy, flat paths to more challenging trails with significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there any easy jogging routes for beginners in Embalse de la Serena?
A: Yes, for beginners or those looking for a less strenuous run, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38158741" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Talarrubias&lt;/a&gt; is an excellent choice. This easy 4.7 km route features minimal elevation gain, making it perfect for a relaxed jog.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails in Embalse de la Serena?
A: The region offers a diverse running experience. You'll encounter vast open spaces, rolling hills, and plains composed of granite, quartzite, or slate. Many paths are interspersed with holm oaks, providing varied scenery throughout the seasons. Some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991754988/penon-del-pez-running-circular-from-penalsordo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñón del Pez – running circular from Peñalsordo&lt;/a&gt;, feature significant elevation changes for a more challenging workout.

Q: Are there any circular running routes around Embalse de la Serena?
A: Yes, many of the routes are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31455189" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado de Santa Ana loop from Peñalsordo&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 13.4 km circular trail that takes you through rolling hills and open landscapes.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I might see while running?
A: While jogging, you can enjoy views of the expansive reservoir itself, often described as an 'inland sea'. A distinctive landmark is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5334061" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Masatrigo&lt;/a&gt;, a perfectly conical inland island. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992985001/cerro-masatrigo-botanical-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Masatrigo Botanical Trail&lt;/a&gt; offers panoramic views of this iconic feature. You might also spot the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5422272" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Serena Dam&lt;/a&gt;, one of Europe's largest.

Q: Is Embalse de la Serena a good place for wildlife spotting during a run?
A: Absolutely. The area is designated as a Special Protection Area for Birds (SPA), making it a fantastic spot for nature observation. While running along the reservoir's edges, you might observe a rich biodiversity, including various aquatic birds and birds of prey. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5414645" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Zújar River&lt;/a&gt;, which feeds the reservoir, also contributes to the diverse ecosystem.

Q: Are there any challenging running trails for experienced runners?
A: Experienced runners will find several challenging options. Five of the seven routes are rated as difficult. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e992985001/cerro-masatrigo-botanical-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Masatrigo Botanical Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, is a difficult 6.8 km route with significant elevation. Another challenging option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31455202" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Capilla and its Castle loop from Peñalsordo&lt;/a&gt;, a 13.7 km difficult trail with notable ascents.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Embalse de la Serena?
A: Runners who have explored Embalse de la Serena often praise the vast open spaces and the unique 'inland sea' feel of the reservoir. The varied terrain, from flat plains to rolling hills and the distinctive Cerro Masatrigo, provides a diverse and engaging experience for those seeking both scenic beauty and a good workout.

Q: Can I find routes of specific lengths, like 10k, in the area?
A: Yes, you can find routes of varying lengths. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31455189" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collado de Santa Ana loop from Peñalsordo&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 13.4 km (approximately 8.3 miles) trail, which is a great option if you're looking for a run around the 10k mark.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Embalse de la Serena?
A: The region's landscape, with its plains and holm oaks, changes color with the seasons, offering different scenic beauty throughout the year. Spring and autumn generally provide milder temperatures, which are ideal for running. Summers can be hot, so early mornings or late evenings are recommended during those months.

Q: Are there any tourist amenities near the running routes?
A: Yes, beyond the running trails, Embalse de la Serena is a popular spot for various outdoor activities. You can find tourist amenities such as beaches, restaurants, and other tourist trails in the vicinity, enhancing the overall visitor experience.
tips: Cerro Masatrigo is so called because it looks like a pile of wheat, it is located in the La Serena reservoir, it is located at an altitude of about 500 meters and with views that are worth it.

faqs.question: How difficult is the Cerro Masatrigo botanical trail (SL-BA 198) for jogging?
faqs.answer: The Komoot tour for the full circular botanical trail (SL-BA 198) is classified as difficult for jogging, with an elevation gain of approximately 167 meters over 6.8 kilometers. While there is a 'low-effort' circular path around the middle of the slope, the route to the summit is very steep and challenging, requiring good fitness and sturdy shoes. The trail is largely exposed, which can add to the difficulty in warmer months.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this jogging trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain on the Cerro Masatrigo botanical trail varies. The circular path around the base is generally less uneven. However, the ascent to the summit features compact earth with some loose stones and is described as very steep. Be prepared for varying surfaces, especially if you plan to tackle the summit section.

faqs.question: What kind of views can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers spectacular 360-degree panoramic views, particularly from the summit of Cerro Masatrigo. You'll see the surrounding waters of the La Serena reservoir, which can make the hill appear like an island when water levels are high. The landscape changes with the seasons, from dry and yellow in summer to lush green in winter and early spring.

faqs.question: What makes Cerro Masatrigo unique?
faqs.answer: Cerro Masatrigo is often called the 'Magic Mountain' due to its striking, almost perfect conical shape. It's a geological wonder and was declared a Natural Monument of Extremadura in May 2023. A distinctive feature is the EX-322 road that encircles the hill, humorously earning it the nickname 'Europe's largest roundabout'.

faqs.question: Where can I park for the Cerro Masatrigo botanical trail?
faqs.answer: Specific parking information for the trailhead is not detailed in the provided sources. However, given the EX-322 road encircles the hill, it is likely that parking is available along this road or at designated points near the start of the trail. We recommend checking local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to access the Cerro Masatrigo Natural Monument?
faqs.answer: The provided information does not indicate any specific permits or entrance fees required to access the Cerro Masatrigo Natural Monument or its botanical trail. It is generally open to the public for outdoor activities.

faqs.question: Is the Cerro Masatrigo botanical trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: The provided information does not explicitly state whether the trail is dog-friendly. As a general rule for natural areas, dogs are often permitted but should be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other visitors. Please check local regulations before bringing your dog.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to jog this trail is during winter and early spring when the landscape is lush green and temperatures are cooler. The trail is largely exposed with little shade, so it can be very hot in summer months, making jogging more challenging. Consider cooler parts of the day if visiting outside of winter/spring.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restrooms near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The provided information does not mention specific facilities such as cafes or restrooms directly at the trailhead. It's advisable to plan accordingly and bring your own water and snacks, and use facilities in nearby towns before or after your run.

faqs.question: Does this trail connect with other routes?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route covers sections of the Camino del Condado de Belalcázar - Etapa 3.

faqs.question: What should I consider regarding sun exposure on the trail?
faqs.answer: The Cerro Masatrigo botanical trail is largely exposed and offers very little shade. This means it can get extremely hot, especially during the summer months. It's crucial to bring plenty of water, wear sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and consider jogging during cooler parts of the day or in the cooler seasons to avoid heat exhaustion.
